sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Supermicro Introduces a Number of Density and Power Optimized Edge Platforms for Telco Providers, Based on the New AMD EPYC™ 8004 Series Processor
feed.summary: Supermicro introduces new H13 WIO family of edge platforms and WIO servers, powered by AMD EPYC™ 8004 Series processors, targeting telco providers. These platforms deliver enhanced performance, energy efficiency, and density, featuring NEBS compliance and AC/DC power options, with TDPs as low as 80 watts for intelligent edge and telco applications. The systems are designed for space-constrained environments, offering PCIe 5.0 and DDR5-4800MHz memory support, making them ideal for edge networks and lowering operational costs.

feed.title: AMDY: YieldMax AMD Option Income Strategy ETF Latest Stock Price, Analysis, News and Trading Ideas
feed.summary: This article provides the latest stock price, analysis, news, and trading ideas for the AMDY: YieldMax AMD Option Income Strategy ETF. It includes current financial metrics such as the stock's price, daily change, after-hours trading, trading volume, 52-week high and low, and average volume. The page also features a sentiment indicator for AMDY and sections for news and discussions.

feed.title: PLDT modernises IT infrastructure to enhance business agility
feed.summary: Philippines telecommunications company PLDT is modernizing its IT infrastructure through a multi-year collaboration with Amdocs, migrating business-critical systems to Amazon Web Services (AWS) to improve business agility and customer experience. Amdocs will provide cloud strategy, security, and operations services using an AI-driven platform, while PLDT will leverage AWS services for application building and infrastructure management. This initiative aims to foster innovation and enhance operational resilience, complementing PLDT Enterprise's existing partnership with Ericsson for AI-enabled solutions.

feed.title: Intel sees massive demand for AI chips designed for China: report
feed.summary: Intel is experiencing high demand for its Gaudi2 AI chips in China, which were designed to comply with US export restrictions. This surge is attributed to Chinese companies developing large language models, leading Intel to increase orders with TSMC. The company's CFO has noted a general trend of customers seeking Gaudi chips as an alternative due to supply shortages.

feed.title: Intel hit with $400 million EU antitrust fine in decades-old case
authors: Foo Yun Chee and Bart H. Meijer
feed.summary: Intel has been fined 376 million euros ($400 million) by the EU for anti-competitive practices nearly two decades ago. This penalty stems from an original 2009 fine for blocking rivals like AMD, which was largely overturned by Europe's second-highest court last year. The new fine specifically addresses Intel's actions between 2002 and 2006, where it paid companies like HP, Acer, and Lenovo to delay or halt competitor products.

feed.title: Oracle spends more than $100 million on Ampere chips
feed.summary: Oracle Corp has agreed to prepay $104.1 million for processor chips from startup Ampere Computing and also invested $400 million in Ampere in its fiscal 2023. This investment and chip purchases are intended to give Oracle's cloud services a competitive edge against rivals like Amazon.com and Google, which develop their own server chips. Ampere designs custom server chips based on Arm technology, competing with Intel and Advanced Micro Devices.

feed.title: AMDY Stock Price | YieldMax AMD Option Income Strategy ETF
feed.summary: This article provides detailed information on the YieldMax AMD Option Income Strategy ETF (AMDY), including its current stock price, performance metrics, key statistics, and holdings. It also covers recent news regarding its distributions and offers technical analysis insights. The fund, launched in September 2023, aims to generate income through options strategies on AMD stock and invests in short-term U.S. treasury securities.

feed.title: AMD Unveils Purpose-Built, FPGA-Based Accelerator for Ultra-Low Latency Electronic Trading
feed.summary: AMD announced the Alveo UL3524 accelerator card, an FPGA-based solution designed for ultra-low latency electronic trading, offering significant speed improvements for financial firms. This new accelerator delivers less than 3ns FPGA transceiver latency and supports AI-enabled trading strategies through its hardware flexibility and the open-sourced FINN development framework. Strategic partners like Alpha Data, Exegy, and Hypertec are already integrating this technology into custom solutions, expanding its ecosystem.

feed.title: Synopsys Appoints Ann Minooka as Chief Marketing Officer
feed.summary: Synopsys, Inc. has announced the appointment of Ann Minooka as its new Chief Marketing Officer, reporting to President and COO Sassine Ghazi. Minooka brings over two decades of marketing leadership experience in the semiconductor industry and will be responsible for Synopsys' global brand strategy, communications, and marketing initiatives. Her previous roles include CMO at Ampere Computing and Xilinx, where she demonstrated a strong track record of strengthening technology brands and driving impactful marketing campaigns.

feed.title: Sony Interactive Entertainment CEO Jim Ryan to Retire in 2024
feed.summary: Jim Ryan, President and CEO of Sony Interactive Entertainment, announced his retirement from the company in March 2024 after almost 30 years. Hiroki Totoki, President and COO of Sony Group Corporation, will serve as interim CEO of SIE starting April 1, 2024. Ryan cited the difficulty of reconciling living in Europe and working in North America as the primary reason for his decision.

feed.title: AMDY Stock Fund Price and Chart — AMEX:AMDY
feed.summary: This article provides an overview of the AMDY YieldMax AMD Option Income Strategy ETF, including its price performance, key statistics like Assets Under Management and dividend yield, and its synthetic covered call strategy. It explains how the fund aims to generate monthly income and provides exposure to AMD stock's price returns, subject to a cap on potential gains, without directly investing in AMD shares. The article also addresses frequently asked questions about ETFs and AMDY's specific characteristics.

feed.title: Top Performing ETFs: Income and Asia ETFs Lead
feed.summary: This article highlights the top-performing ETFs of the past week, focusing on income-generating and Asia-focused ETFs. The Simplify Interest Rate Hedge ETF (PFIX) led, demonstrating investor interest in strategies to adapt to potential rate hikes. Additionally, several YieldMax income strategies and the KraneShares SSE Star Market 50 Index ETF (KSTR), which focuses on Chinese science and technology firms, showed strong performance.

feed.title: Top Performing ETFs: Income and Asia ETFs on Top
feed.summary: The article highlights top-performing ETFs, with a focus on income and Asia-centric funds. The Simplify Interest Rate Hedge ETF (PFIX B) led the gains, while several YieldMax strategies focused on income also performed well. Additionally, the KraneShares SSE Star Market 50 Index ETF (KSTR B-), which targets Chinese science and technology firms, showed strong performance.

feed.title: UT’s Texas Institute for Electronics and Infleqtion Launch Quantum Manufacturing Center of Excellence
feed.summary: The University of Texas at Austin and Infleqtion have partnered to establish a new center of excellence for quantum manufacturing, named qNexus. This collaboration aims to scale the domestic production of quantum-enabled products for various sectors, including energy, defense, and healthcare, while also developing the future workforce for quantum and photonic manufacturing. The initiative is supported by significant funding from the Texas Legislature and builds on UT's expertise in quantum materials and algorithms.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc: Will AMD Stock Price Reach $120 Mark?
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock has been trading in a declining parallel channel since June 2023, despite a 56.47% year-to-date gain. The stock is attempting to recover above its 20-EMA and is targeting the $120 mark if it can break out of its current falling pattern. Technical indicators suggest a struggle, with investors needing to see increased buyer accumulation for a sustained recovery.

feed.title: Elevate efficiency in converged infrastructure | NetApp Blog
feed.summary: The article discusses how the FlexPod converged infrastructure, integrating NetApp, Cisco, and AMD technologies, can revolutionize IT efficiency. It highlights the benefits of this trio, combining all-flash storage, fortified Cisco compute, and powerful AMD EPYC processors, to deliver enhanced performance, cost savings, and simplified management for enterprise applications and VDI solutions. The integration aims to streamline data management, provide powerful computing, and enable rapid deployment and scaling through automation.

feed.title: NVIDIA Omniverse is Changing Filmmaking and Exxact is Helping It Happen
feed.summary: The article highlights how NVIDIA Omniverse and Exxact Corporation are revolutionizing filmmaking, especially in animation and VFX, by enabling real-time virtual production. It showcases how companies like Sony Pictures Animation, disguise, and Versatile Media are using Omniverse to improve workflows, accelerate production, and facilitate remote collaboration. Exxact Corporation provides integrated, high-performance computing solutions to help these companies adopt Omniverse Enterprise.

feed.title: AI Accelerated Video Editing with DaVinci Resolve 18.6 & AMD Radeon Graphics
feed.summary: AMD and Blackmagic Design collaborated to optimize DaVinci Resolve 18.6 for AMD hardware, significantly boosting AI feature performance. This optimization, specifically for AMD Radeon™ RX 7900 XTX graphics cards, leads to up to 4x faster neural engine performance in video editing tasks like Magic Mask, Super Scale, and Speed Warp, enhancing the content creation experience. The improvements leverage the Microsoft DirectML platform API to accelerate AI workloads.

feed.title: Supermicro Accelerates Everything at ISC23
feed.summary: Supermicro showcased its latest High-Performance Computing (HPC) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) solutions at ISC23 in Hamburg, Germany, emphasizing its leadership in AI infrastructure. Key highlights included liquid-cooled NVIDIA HGX H100 GPU servers, an AI development platform with NVIDIA, and an 8U PVC system featuring Intel Data Series GPUs. The company also presented high-performance storage solutions optimized for AI workloads, demonstrating its commitment to accelerating AI adoption.

feed.title: HPE delivers Austral supercomputer to CRIANN in France
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has delivered a new AI supercomputer named Austral to CRIANN in France. This 2 petaflops system, co-funded by the Normandy Region, French State, and EU, will be used for AI model training and simulations in various fields such as climate modeling, biotechnology, and healthcare. Austral features AMD Genoa, Intel Cooper Lake cores, and Nvidia A100 GPUs, and is hosted in a customized HPE HPC pod with direct liquid cooling.

feed.title: Nvidia and AMD reportedly developing Arm-based PC processors
feed.summary: Nvidia and AMD are reportedly developing new Arm-based PC processors, aiming to enter the Windows computer market as early as 2025. This move aligns with Microsoft's efforts to foster competition and introduce more energy-efficient CPUs with advanced AI features, similar to Apple's Arm-based Macs. Qualcomm currently holds an exclusivity agreement with Microsoft for Arm-based Windows chips, which is set to expire by the end of the year, potentially opening the door for Nvidia and AMD.

feed.title: Nvidia to take on Intel, Qualcomm with Arm-based processors for PC; availability pegged for 2025
feed.summary: Nvidia is developing Arm-based processors for personal computers, aiming to compete with Intel and Qualcomm, with availability expected as soon as 2025. This move intensifies competition in the PC chip market, especially as AMD is also reportedly working on Arm-based processors for PCs. The initiative follows Apple's successful transition to in-house Arm-based chips, boosting Mac sales, and poses a significant challenge to Intel's dominant position.

feed.title: Micron Announces Sampling of 9.6 Gbps LPDDR5X Memory
feed.summary: Micron Technology announced it is shipping production samples of its 1β (1-beta) mobile-optimized LPDDR5X memory, operating at a record 9.6 Gbps. This memory is designed for use with Qualcomm's Snapdragon 8 Gen 3 mobile platform to enable generative AI at the edge. It offers enhanced performance and power efficiency crucial for on-device AI applications, reducing reliance on cloud-based solutions.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. stock underperforms Wednesday when compared to competitors
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) shares fell by 5.52% on Wednesday to $96.06, underperforming the broader stock market, which also experienced a rough trading session. The S&P 500 Index dropped 1.43%, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average declined 0.32%. AMD's closing price was $36.78 below its 52-week high of $132.83, achieved in June.

feed.title: Microsoft will hold a 27% stake in OpenAI and gain access to its AI models.
feed.summary: Microsoft and OpenAI have finalized a new agreement where Microsoft will acquire a 27% stake in OpenAI, valued at $135 billion. This agreement grants Microsoft access to OpenAI's AI technology, including AGI models, until 2032. OpenAI has also committed to investing an additional $250 billion in purchasing Azure services.

feed.title: AMD soars as AI chip sales prediction bodes well for rivalry with Nvidia
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares jumped 8% after CEO Lisa Su provided an upbeat 2024 sales forecast of over $2 billion for its MI300 AI chips, positioning AMD as a clear second source to Nvidia in the AI accelerator market. This prediction helped alleviate concerns about a disappointing fourth-quarter forecast despite at least 18 analysts cutting price targets due to conservative forecasts and weakness in other AMD markets. The MI300 is set to become AMD's fastest product to reach $1 billion in sales, having secured commitments from multiple large hyperscale customers.

feed.title: Up 35% This Year, How Will Applied Materials Stock Trend Following Q4 Results?
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) is set to release its Q4 FY’23 results on November 16, with expectations of a 9% year-over-year revenue decline due to weaker memory market demand. Despite strong year-to-date gains of 35% for AMAT stock, concerns remain about potential underperformance against the S&P 500 given the uncertain macroeconomic environment and slowing semiconductor equipment budgets. However, the company's valuation is seen as reasonable, and long-term growth drivers like generative AI suggest future upside.

feed.title: Automotive Digitalization Sparks Big Growth For Chips And Design Tools
feed.summary: The automotive industry is experiencing significant growth in semiconductor dependency due to innovations like autonomous driving, advanced driver-assist features, and infotainment systems. This surge is driven by the adoption of a "software-defined vehicle" concept and a scalable, zonal digital chassis architecture. Companies like Cadence Design Systems are playing a crucial role by providing tools for chip design, system modeling, simulation, and multiphysics analysis to support this technological transformation.

feed.title: Streamlining SoC Integration With the Power of Automation
feed.summary: This article discusses the increasing complexity of System-on-Chip (SoC) integration and the critical need for automation to manage this complexity. It highlights how the IP-XACT standard and tools like Magillem from Arteris provide a standardized, automated approach to streamline SoC integration, reduce errors, and accelerate time-to-market. The piece emphasizes that automation is essential for successful SoC design, improving productivity and overall effectiveness.

feed.title: Raspberry Pi Receives Strategic Investment from Arm, Further Extending Long-Term Partnership
feed.summary: Arm Holdings plc has made a strategic minority investment in Raspberry Pi Ltd, deepening their partnership that began in 2008. This investment aims to further enable the Internet of Things (IoT) developer community with low-cost, high-performance computing solutions built on Arm technology, particularly as edge compute and AI applications grow. Both companies share a vision to democratize technology and remove barriers to entry for innovation across various user groups.

feed.title: REX FANG & Innovation Equity Premium Income ETF
feed.summary: This article provides a comprehensive financial overview of the REX FANG & Innovation Equity Premium Income ETF (FEPI), including its current price, dividend information, expense ratio, and top holdings. It also details the fund's ESG scores, performance metrics, geographic and sector exposure, and technical analysis, offering a detailed profile for potential investors.

feed.title: Intel CEO Admits to and Details The Company's Three Biggest Mistakes
feed.summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger openly discussed what he considers Intel's three biggest missteps: the failed smartphone business, the cancellation of the early AI-oriented GPU Larrabee, and the historical lack of focus on building a robust foundry. He believes these errors led to a disadvantage in the current AI market and impacted the company's overall direction. Gelsinger is now driving Intel's IDM 2.0 initiative, opening its fabs to external customers to generate revenue and invest in manufacturing and R&D.

feed.title: On-device AI chips: New battleground for chipmakers
feed.summary: Global chipmakers like Samsung Electronics, Qualcomm, Intel, and AMD are intensifying their focus on on-device AI chips for smartphones, laptops, and PCs, recognizing its potential for customized services, lower costs, and enhanced privacy compared to generative AI. This shift is expected to drive significant growth in AI-enabled consumer electronics and contribute to rising memory chip prices. Both on-device AI and generative AI are seen as complementary, with companies like Samsung planning to integrate both into future smart devices.
feed.source: The Korea Economic Daily Global Edition
feed.source_domain: The Korea Economic Daily Global Edition

feed.title: Penguin Solutions to Showcase Expertise in AI and HPC at SC23
feed.summary: Penguin Solutions will exhibit its AI and High-Performance Computing (HPC) solutions at Supercomputing 2023 (SC23) in Denver from November 12-17. The company's booth will feature technology demonstrations on designing, building, deploying, and managing AI/HPC infrastructures, including insights into immersion cooling and hybrid cloud solutions. Penguin experts will also participate in speaking engagements discussing AI factories and sustainable deployment environments like immersion cooling.

feed.title: Cadence open-source PDK and reference design now available for SkyWater's 130nm process
feed.summary: SkyWater Technology has announced that Cadence Design Systems has created an open-source 130 nm Process Design Kit (PDK) for their SKY130 process, which will be included in the Cadence VLSI Fundamentals Education Kit. This initiative aims to remove barriers to semiconductor design for academia and researchers by providing accessible tools without NDAs. The collaboration between SkyWater and Cadence is expected to accelerate collaboration in chip design, support semiconductor education, and strengthen the domestic semiconductor workforce.

feed.title: Micron Announces 128GB DRAM Low-Latency, High-Capacity RDIMMs
feed.summary: Micron Technology has launched new 128 GB DDR5 RDIMM memory modules featuring 32Gb monolithic die technology, offering best-in-class performance up to 8000 MT/s. These high-capacity, low-latency modules are designed for data center and cloud environments, particularly for AI, in-memory databases, and high-performance computing, providing significant improvements in bit density, energy efficiency, latency, and AI training performance over competitive products. AMD and Intel have expressed interest in these new RDIMMs for their latest server platforms due to the enhanced capacity and bandwidth benefits.

feed.title: After Earnings, Is AMD Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
feed.summary: Morningstar assesses AMD's stock post-Q3 earnings, evaluating its fair value, economic moat, and future prospects, particularly in the AI accelerator market. The company's target of over $2 billion in AI accelerator revenue for 2024 positions it as a significant player. While fair value is estimated at $125 per share, high uncertainty exists due to competition from Nvidia and potential shifts in the x86 architecture dominance.

feed.title: New AMD Radeon PRO Workstation Graphics Card to Power Next-Generation Professional Content Creation, CAD and AI Applications
feed.summary: AMD has launched the Radeon PRO W7700, a new workstation graphics card designed for professional content creation, CAD, and AI applications. Priced under $1,000, it offers significant performance increases in key professional software compared to competitors and features 16GB of VRAM and DisplayPort 2.1. The card also includes hardware-based AI accelerators to speed up AI workloads in various applications.

feed.title: TTM Technologies to Build $130 Million Advanced Circuit Board Facility
feed.summary: TTM Technologies is set to construct a new $130 million advanced circuit board facility in DeWitt, New York, adjacent to its existing plant. The facility will specialize in ultra-high density interconnect PCBs for semiconductor and national security applications, creating approximately 400 jobs. New York State will support the project with up to $17 million in tax incentives and a $5 million grant for equipment.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. stock outperforms market on strong trading day
feed.summary: Shares of Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) rose by 2.65% to $119.88 on Tuesday, outperforming the broader market. This positive performance occurred on a strong trading day where the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average also saw significant gains. The stock closed below its 52-week high of $132.83, which was achieved on June 13th.

feed.title: AMD-powered Frontier system wins first place in Top500 supercomputer ranking
feed.summary: The Top500 computing organization has again named Frontier, an AMD-powered system at Oak Ridge National Laboratory, as the world's fastest supercomputer, achieving 1.194 exaflops. This marks the system's continued dominance since June, showcasing its high performance and impressive power efficiency. The ranking also highlights the rise of other powerful systems like Aurora and Eagle, while demonstrating AMD's growing presence in the supercomputing sector.

feed.title: AMD Brings New AI and Compute Capabilities to Microsoft Customers
feed.summary: AMD and Microsoft are collaborating to integrate AMD's AI and compute technologies into Microsoft's cloud and PC ecosystems. This includes the AMD Instinct MI300X accelerator powering new Azure AI-optimized virtual machines and 4th Gen AMD EPYC processors enhancing general-purpose, memory-intensive, and compute-optimized VMs. Additionally, AMD is expanding its Ryzen AI presence in PCs for generative AI experiences.

feed.title: SK's Sapeon launches new AI chip for data centers
feed.summary: SK's AI chip designer Sapeon has unveiled its new X330 neural processing unit (NPU) chip, targeting data centers and challenging Nvidia. The X330, produced by TSMC and set for mass production in early 2024, offers significant improvements in arithmetic capability and energy efficiency over its predecessor. Sapeon aims to make advanced AI technology more accessible and plans to expand its chip intellectual properties for autonomous vehicles and other advanced devices.

feed.title: AMD Is Powering the New Steam Machine. Will It Move the Needle for AMD Stock?
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) will power Valve's new Steam Machine with its RDNA 3 GPU and Zen 4 CPU, a development that reinforces AMD's reputation as an innovator and aids in brand visibility and diversification, despite being unlikely to significantly impact AMD stock revenue immediately. The article discusses AMD's strong Q3 earnings, high valuation multiples compared to competitors like Nvidia, Qualcomm, and Texas Instruments, and analyst price targets. While AI dominates current chipmaker focus, the gaming industry remains crucial, showcasing AMD's continued innovation in the sector.

feed.title: The 3 Hottest ETFs to Buy Right Now
feed.summary: This article highlights three top-performing ETFs for investors: the Invesco S&P 500 Top 50 ETF (XLG), the iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX), and the Vanguard Information Technology ETF (VGT). It details their holdings, expense ratios, and strong historical performance compared to the broader market, making a case for their inclusion in a long-term investment portfolio.

feed.title: USD ETF | ProShares Ultra Semiconductors 2X Shares
feed.summary: This article provides detailed financial information for the ProShares Ultra Semiconductors 2X Shares (ARCA:USD) ETF. It lists current market data such as price, PE and PB ratios, volume, and dividend yield. The article also highlights top holdings including IQMM, NVDA, AVGO, MU, and AMD, and indicates the ETF's turnover rate and total value.

feed.title: Gaming PCs under $1k: Black Friday makes gaming more budget-friendly at Newegg
feed.summary: This article highlights several Black Friday deals on gaming PCs available at Newegg, all priced under $1,000. It presents three specific pre-built options from MXZ, Skytech, and IPASON, detailing their specifications, discounted prices, and suitability for various gaming needs. The article emphasizes that these deals make PC gaming more accessible for budget-conscious consumers and advises checking Newegg's full selection or using their PC Finder.

feed.title: Is Nvidia Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued After Earnings?
feed.summary: Nvidia's recent earnings exceeded expectations, driven by strong demand for its GPUs in the AI sector, though the company anticipates a decline in China revenue due to U.S. export restrictions. Morningstar maintains a "fairly valued" assessment for Nvidia stock with a fair value estimate of $480 per share, acknowledging its wide economic moat from intangible assets and switching costs. Despite a Very High Uncertainty Rating due to competition and potential reliance reduction by hyperscale vendors, Nvidia is expected to remain a market leader in AI semiconductors for years.

feed.title: Market Leaders Collaborate with Synopsys to Realize Gains of Generative AI Across Synopsys.ai Full EDA Stack
feed.summary: Synopsys announced the expansion of its Synopsys.ai EDA suite to integrate generative AI capabilities across its full stack, aiming to significantly enhance engineering productivity for chipmakers. This initiative, starting with Synopsys.ai Copilot, builds on its existing AI-driven optimization solutions. Leading companies like AMD, Intel, and Microsoft are collaborating with Synopsys to leverage these GenAI tools for various aspects of chip design, from RTL creation to formal verification.

feed.title: Is Automotive Embedded Systems Market Really a Strong Market to Invest in?
feed.summary: Advance Market Analytics has released a new research report on the "Automotive Embedded Systems Market Insights, to 2028." The study highlights the market's significant expansion driven by increasing R&D spending and identifies key players such as Robert Bosch, Continental, and Infineon Technologies. The report covers market trends like AI and Machine Learning integration, opportunities in electric vehicles, and drivers such as consumer awareness of vehicle safety.

feed.title: SoC Integration And Data Transport Architecture Requirements Surge In 2023
feed.summary: The article reviews 2023's trends in SoC integration and data transport architectures, highlighting the significant acceleration of AI/ML and its impact on chip design. It discusses the growing complexity of SoCs and NoC protocols, the rise of chiplets, and future requirements for standardization, safety, and security in 2024.

feed.title: Apple to become largest customer of new $2B chip packaging facility in Arizona
feed.summary: Apple announced it will be the primary customer for Amkor Technology Inc.'s new $2 billion chip packaging facility in Arizona, which will package chips produced by Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) at a nearby complex. This facility will play a crucial role in assembling and protecting the next generation of processors for various Apple products. The investment underscores Apple's commitment to American manufacturing and will create over 2,000 jobs.

feed.title: ASML boss Wennink to retire in April; veteran Fouquet to step up
feed.summary: ASML Chief Executive Peter Wennink will retire in April 2024 after overseeing a decade of significant growth, during which the company became Europe's largest technology firm. He will be succeeded by company veteran Christophe Fouquet, who has extensive experience with ASML's critical extreme ultraviolet (EUV) product lines. Additionally, Chief Technology Officer Martin van den Brink will also retire, and Jim Koonmen will join the management board as Chief Customer Officer.

feed.title: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) Morgan Stanley Raises Target Forecast
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has increased its 12-month target price for NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) from $235.00 to $250.00, while re-iterating an "Overweight" rating. This upgrade indicates strong confidence in NVIDIA's fundamentals, driven by AI and data-center demand, despite market concerns about U.S.–China export restrictions and competitive pressures. The target hike acts as a counterweight to bearish sentiment, suggesting the recent stock pullback may be overdone relative to NVIDIA’s long-term earnings potential.

feed.title: AI Alliance Launches as an International Community of Leading Technology Developers, Researchers, and Adopters Collaborating Together to Advance Open, Safe, Responsible AI
feed.summary: The AI Alliance has launched as an international community of over 50 founding members, including IBM and Meta, to advance open, safe, and responsible AI. The alliance aims to foster open innovation and science in AI by bringing together diverse organizations to accelerate responsible AI development, establish benchmarks, and build a vibrant AI ecosystem. Its main goal is to ensure that AI advancements are harnessed in ways that prioritize safety, diversity, economic opportunity, and benefits for all, addressing societal challenges and promoting ethical AI practices.

feed.title: Meta Platforms, IBM create industrywide AI alliance to share technology
feed.summary: Meta Platforms and IBM are leading an industry-wide AI Alliance of over 40 companies and organizations to promote open-source artificial intelligence development. The coalition aims to share technology, reduce risks, and focus on responsible AI development, including safety and security tools. This initiative seeks to increase open-source AI models and foster collaboration with academic researchers.

feed.title: AMD's highly anticipated MI300X AI chips hit general availability along with new Ryzen 8040 CPUs
feed.summary: AMD announced the general availability of its new Instinct MI300X series data center AI accelerators and the Ryzen 8040 Series chips for smaller devices, marking a significant push into the AI market to challenge Nvidia. The MI300X series focuses on generative AI workloads and HPC performance, supported by launch partners like Microsoft, Oracle, and Meta, while the Ryzen 8040 CPUs bring AI capabilities directly to personal computers. AMD also introduced the ROCm 6 open software stack and previewed next-generation "Strix Point" CPUs, emphasizing AI as a top priority.

feed.title: Supermicro Extends AI and GPU Rack Scale Solutions with Support for AMD Instinct MI300 Series Accelerators
feed.summary: Supermicro is expanding its AI and GPU rack scale solutions by introducing three new H13 generation GPU servers that support the AMD Instinct MI300 Series accelerators. These new servers, including 8-GPU systems with the MI300X OAM configuration and 2U liquid-cooled and 4U air-cooled servers with the MI300A APUs, are designed to deliver enhanced performance and efficiency for large-scale AI training, LLM deployments, and HPC applications. Supermicro emphasizes its ability to deliver complete liquid-cooled racks and its optimized building block architecture to meet demanding AI and supercomputing needs.

feed.title: Opinion: AMD’s new products represent the first real threat to Nvidia’s AI dominance
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc.'s new product launch, specifically its Instinct MI300 family of GPUs and ROCm software ecosystem, is seen by analysts as the first serious challenge to Nvidia Corp.'s dominance in the artificial intelligence market. These new offerings are designed to accelerate AI applications and directly compete with Nvidia's CUDA platform.

feed.title: AMD releases new chips to power faster AI training
feed.summary: AMD announced the availability of its new MI300 chips, including the Instinct MI300X accelerator and Instinct M1300A APU, designed to power demanding AI training and large language models. The company also unveiled the Ryzen 8040 mobile processors, which will bring enhanced AI capabilities to laptops in 2024. These releases position AMD to compete more directly with Nvidia in the booming AI chip market.

feed.title: Tesla’s Dojo Supercomputer Head Exits in Blow to Efforts
feed.summary: Ganesh Venkataramanan, the head of Tesla's Dojo supercomputer project, has left the company after five years, posing a significant setback to the automaker's self-driving technology endeavors. Peter Bannon, a long-time Tesla director and former Apple executive, has taken over leadership of the project. This departure comes as Tesla plans to invest over $1 billion in Dojo by the end of 2024, a project analysts believe could add $500 billion to Tesla's valuation.

feed.title: Comparing Analog Devices With Industry Competitors In Semiconductors & Semiconductor Equipment Industry
feed.summary: This article provides a comprehensive comparison of Analog Devices (NASDAQ:ADI) against its competitors in the Semiconductors & Semiconductor Equipment industry, analyzing key financial metrics and market position. While Analog Devices shows potential undervaluation based on its low P/E, P/B, and P/S ratios, it also exhibits lower efficiency, profitability, and revenue growth compared to industry averages. The analysis highlights both the company's strengths, such as a strong financial position with a low debt-to-equity ratio, and areas of concern like weaker financial performance metrics.

feed.title: AMD debuts new chip with eye to rival Nvidia product
feed.summary: AMD has unveiled its new MI300 accelerator chips, aiming to compete with Nvidia in the burgeoning artificial intelligence (AI) chip market. CEO Lisa Su projected the AI chip industry could exceed $400 billion in the next four years, and mentioned Microsoft, Oracle, and Meta Platforms as customers. The company claims its new chip outperforms Nvidia's H100 in AI inference and matches it in training capabilities.

feed.title: Themes Generative Artificial Intelligence ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:WISE)
feed.summary: The Themes Generative Artificial Intelligence ETF (WISE) tracks an index of companies involved in AI-related industries, with a concentrated portfolio of 89 holdings. Launched on December 8, 2023, the ETF currently has $33.55 million in assets under management and a net expense ratio of 0.35%. Its top holdings include major tech companies like Advanced Micro Devices, Intel, Apple, and NVIDIA.

feed.title: STMicroelectronics speeds up Edge AI adoption for product transformation
feed.summary: STMicroelectronics has launched the ST Edge AI Suite, a free software suite aimed at simplifying the integration of Edge AI into products using ST hardware. This initiative seeks to accelerate the adoption of AI at the edge, offering tools like NanoEdge AI Studio freely for STM32 and other ARM Cortex-M microcontrollers. The suite supports various applications, from enhancing appliance efficiency to optimizing EV motors, and is expected to be released in the first half of 2024.

feed.title: AMD launches MI300, claims AI performance lead over Nvidia ...
feed.summary: AMD has launched its Instinct MI300 series data center AI accelerators and ROCm 6 software stack, claiming a significant performance advantage over Nvidia's H100. The MI300 series comes in two variants, MI300x and MI300A, offering substantial memory and processing power for AI and high-performance computing. AMD highlights strong demand and adoption by major tech companies, positioning the MI300 as a leading solution for generative AI.

feed.title: Silicon Labs in search of new CFO
feed.summary: Silicon Laboratories Inc. is looking for a new Chief Financial Officer after John Hollister announced his departure on February 24 to join GlobalFoundries. The Austin-based company will be seeking a new CFO, and this report also includes ABJ's latest list of the largest locally based public companies.

feed.title: Cooperation with ASML in Netherlands becomes important for AI chip war
feed.summary: The article highlights the growing importance of the Netherlands, specifically ASML, in the global semiconductor supply chain, especially with the surge in demand for AI chips. South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol's visit, accompanied by top executives from Samsung and SK Group, underscores the critical need for cooperation with ASML, the sole manufacturer of essential photolithography systems. This alliance is seen as crucial for the success of the South Korea-U.S.-Japan semiconductor partnership as the high-performing chip market expands rapidly due to AI.

feed.title: TSMC Global Market Share Rises by 2% QoQ, Samsung, UMC, GlobalFoundries Saw 1% QoQ Decline
feed.summary: TSMC increased its global market share by 2% quarter-over-quarter in Q3 2023, reaching 59% due to recovering markets in PCs and smartphones. Conversely, Samsung Foundry, UMC, and GlobalFoundries each experienced a 1% decline in market share, while SMIC maintained its share. The report also highlights strong demand for AI chips and anticipated inventory corrections continuing into Q1 2024.

feed.title: Lisa Su’s net worth: How she saved AMD & built her wealth
feed.summary: This article details Lisa Su's background, her impact as CEO of Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), and how she built her net worth. It covers her engineering career, her strategies to diversify AMD's product portfolio and financial recovery, and her compensation, highlighting that her wealth primarily comes from AMD stock holdings. The article also touches upon AMD's stock performance under her leadership and her status relative to being a billionaire.

feed.title: Sony at CES2024 - The World's Largest Technology Event - Sony Group Portal
feed.summary: Sony will be exhibiting at CES 2024, the world's largest technology event, showcasing initiatives that support creators. The company has a long history with CES, dating back to 1977, and has evolved its presence from a consumer electronics trade show to a platform for introducing cutting-edge technologies. A press conference will be streamed on January 8, 2024, at 5:00 p.m. PST, detailing their announcements.
